title: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
repair_hint: Normalize and resolve paths, reject traversal, and ensure the final path stays inside the allowed base directory.

title: External Control of File Name or Path
repair_hint: Do not use user-controlled file paths directly. Validate, normalize, and restrict them to allowed directories.

title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
repair_hint: Avoid shell=True and shell string interpolation. Use subprocess argument lists.

title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
repair_hint: Avoid command strings with user input. Use argument lists and validate arguments.

title: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
repair_hint: Avoid eval/exec or dynamic code generation with user-controlled data.

title: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
repair_hint: Validate redirect targets and allow only same-origin or explicitly allowed redirects.

title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
repair_hint: Use parameterized SQL queries and pass user input as bound parameters.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
repair_hint: Validate outbound URLs, restrict schemes/hosts, and block private/internal IP ranges.

title: Improper Input Validation
repair_hint: Validate input type, length, range, format, and allowed values before use.

title: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
repair_hint: Avoid exposing sensitive information in responses, errors, logs, or timing differences.

title: Improper Privilege Management
repair_hint: Enforce privilege checks before sensitive operations.

title: Missing Authorization
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Improper Authorization
repair_hint: Add explicit authorization checks before protected actions.

title: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges
repair_hint: Apply least privilege and avoid unnecessary elevated permissions.

title: Incorrect Authorization
repair_hint: Verify roles, permissions, and object ownership before sensitive operations.

title: Improper Authentication
repair_hint: Verify authentication state server-side before granting access.

title: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
repair_hint: Check ownership and authorization before accessing user-controlled object IDs.

title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
repair_hint: Avoid unsafe deserialization of untrusted data.

title: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
repair_hint: Ensure resources are only exposed to the correct security boundary or authorized actor.

title: Improper Access Control
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
repair_hint: Redact sensitive values before logging.

title: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
repair_hint: Use secure tempfile APIs and avoid following attacker-controlled symbolic links.

title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
repair_hint: Do not expose credentials in logs, URLs, errors, or responses.

title: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
repair_hint: Validate uploaded file type, size, extension, and storage path.

title: Insufficient Session Expiration
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
repair_hint: Require authentication before sensitive endpoints or operations.

title: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
repair_hint: Verify signatures against the correct signed object and trusted keys.

title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
repair_hint: Require and validate CSRF tokens for state-changing requests.

title: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Observable Discrepancy
repair_hint: Use generic responses and constant-time comparisons where appropriate.

title: Origin Validation Error
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere
repair_hint: Identify the user-controlled input and the vulnerable sink. Apply the smallest safe validation, escaping, authorization, or bounds check. Preserve existing behavior and avoid unrelated rewrites.

title: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
repair_hint: Handle exceptional paths explicitly and avoid hangs or infinite loops.

title: Out-of-bounds Write
repair_hint: Check buffer sizes and bounds before writing.
